docs/NetDocs.html.summary: NetDocuments Event handling Calling the NetDocuments saveAs dialog via their ComAddin.Object means that it runs synchronously so need something like System.Windows.Forms.Application.DoEvents. Calling it via the Word Application.CommandBars.ExecuteMso "FileSaveAs" it runs asynchronously. Save as new or save as existing document sequenceDiagram participant u as User participant nd as NetDocuments participant w as Word u->>nd: Save Document nd->>nd: NdBeforeBrowseEvent Note over nd: Dialog shows and on exit nd->>nd: NdSaveDialogResultEvent - Result 0 nd->>nd: NdBeforeSaveEvent nd->>nd: NdEditProfileEvent w->>w: DocumentBeforeSave w->>nd: DocumentBeforeSave nd->>nd: NdBeforeSaveEvent Save as new or save as existing document with cancel sequenceDiagram participant u as User participant nd as NetDocuments participant w as Word u->>nd: Save Document nd->>nd: NdBeforeBrowseEvent Note over nd: Dialog shows and on exit nd->>nd: NdSaveDialogResultEvent - Result 1 Open sequenceDiagram participant u as User participant nd as NetDocuments participant w as Word u->>nd: Open Document w->>nd: DocumentOpen nd->>nd: ndDocumentReadyEvent w->>w: DocumentChange w->>w: DocumentOpen Close and save sequenceDiagram participant u as User participant nd as NetDocuments participant w as Word u->>nd: Close Document nd->>nd: NdUnsavedChangesResultEvent - Result 2 nd->>nd: NdBeforeSaveEvent nd->>nd: NdEditProfileEvent w->>w: DocumentBeforeSave w->>nd: DocumentBeforeSave nd->>nd: NdBeforeSaveEvent w->>w: DocumentBeforeClose w->>w: DocumentBeforeSave There are only 5 events Event Description NdAfterSaveEvent Occurs after ndOffice Save. NdBeforeBrowseEvent Occurs before Save As dialog appears Remarks Using this event you can possibly change default profile attributes for new documents to show on the Save As dialog. NdBeforeSaveEvent Occurs before ndOffice Save. NdDocumentReadyEvent Occurs when user document was opened and ready for processing NdEditProfileEvent Occurs before ND document content saved inside echo location. Remarks Using this event you can change profile attributes after dialogs and update document NdEditProfileResultEvent Occurs when user edits profile attributes in NdSetProfile event. (0 for OK 1 for cancel) NdSaveDialogResultEvent Occurs when user closes save dialog. (0 for OK 1 for cancel) NdUnsavedChangesResultEvent Occurs when prompt about unsaved changes is closed. (0 Cancel, 1 don't save)

docs/configuration.html.title: Configuration | Developer Docs
docs/configuration.html.summary: Configuration The Office Add-ins can be configured using various JSON configuration files. The location of the Configuration files is typically within the AppData Folders. Changing the Configuration Location There is an appsettings.json file that can be used to change the location of the configuration files. The file is located in the Installation folder of the Add-in, typically C:\Program Files (x86)\Kutana+Brochet\Word\. { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Default": "Information", } }, "ConfigurationLocations": { "Shared": "%appdata%/kutana+brochet/Word/Config", "User": "%localappdata%/kutana+brochet/Word/Config" } }

docs/document-menus.html.title: Document Menus - Developer Guide | Developer Docs
docs/document-menus.html.summary: Document Menus - Developer Guide Technical implementation guide for the New Document, Starter Document, and Insert Snippet dynamic menu subsystem. All three features share a common discovery, formatting, and rendering pipeline but differ in their final action. Menu Type Action Creates New Document? Default Extensions New Document CreateDocument Yes (from template) .dotx, .dotm Starter Document CreateFromStarterDocument Yes (from existing doc) .docx, .docm, .dotx, .dotm Insert Snippet InsertSnippet No (inserts into active doc) .docx, .docm Architecture Overview The document menu subsystem implements a pipeline pattern: file discovery, menu formatting, XML rendering, and command dispatch. Ribbon click (dynamicMenu getContent callback) | v GetDocumentMenuItems.Query --> FileProviderRepository --> FileDiscoveryService (reads config) (scans file system) | v FormatDocumentMenu.Query --> FolderTreeBuilder + DocumentMenuFormatter | v DocumentMenuXmlRenderer --> XML string returned to Office | v User picks item --> Ribbon onAction callback | v RibbonTagCache.TryGet(tag) --> full file path | v CreateDocument / CreateDocumentFromStarterDocument / InsertSnippet command Key Architectural Principles Shared Pipeline: All three menu types use the same discovery, formatting, and rendering infrastructure Menu-Type Discrimination: DocumentMenuType enum distinguishes behaviour at each pipeline stage Configuration-Driven: Menu content is entirely driven by FileProviderConfiguration JSON Tag-Based Security: File paths are never exposed in ribbon XML; RibbonTagCache maps opaque tags to paths Clean Architecture: Domain has no infrastructure dependencies; COM interop is isolated in the VSTO layer Layer Structure Domain Layer (Core.Domain.Word) IDocumentSource Base abstraction for any file that can appear in a menu: public interface IDocumentSource { string Name { get; } // Filename (e.g. "Contract.dotx") string Location { get; } // Full path string Container { get; } // Parent folder path string Extension { get; } bool Matches(string value); bool MatchesContainer(IDocumentSource source); } DocumentFile Sealed record implementing IDocumentSource for files on disk. Created via factory method: public static Maybe<DocumentFile> Create(string path, bool exists) Returns Maybe.None if the file does not exist. DocumentPath Validated path value object: public sealed record DocumentPath { public static Result<DocumentPath, Error> Create(string path) } Rejects null/whitespace paths with DomainErrors.Document.InvalidPath. Template Entity representing a Word template (.dotx/.dotm). Extends TemplateReference: public record Template(IDocumentSource Source, TemplateMetadata Metadata) Key behaviours: MatchesFilter(string) - regex match against file name (used for pre-profile configuration) IsMissing() - true if Source is a MissingDocument StarterDocument Pairs a source file with its resolved template: public record StarterDocument(IDocumentSource Source, Template Template) The Template is resolved by inspecting the starter file's XML to read its AttachedTemplate property. Application Layer (Core.Application) DocumentMenuType public enum DocumentMenuType { NewDocument, StarterDocument, Snippet } MenuAction Immutable record mapping menu types to ribbon callback names: public static readonly MenuAction CreateDocument = new("CreateDocument", "FileNewBlankDocument"); public static readonly MenuAction CreateFromStarterDocument = new("CreateFromStarterDocument", "FileNewBlankDocument"); public static readonly MenuAction InsertSnippet = new("InsertSnippet", "FileNewBlankDocument"); The ActionName is written into the generated ribbon XML as the onAction attribute value. GetDocumentMenuItems.Query Retrieves raw file list for a given menu button ID: public class Query(DocumentMenuType menuType, string id) : IQuery Returns IReadOnlyList from IFileProviderRepository.GetDocuments(). FormatDocumentMenu.Query Transforms raw documents into a structured DocumentMenu: public class Query(DocumentMenuType menuType, IReadOnlyList<IDocumentSource> documents) : IQuery Delegates to IDocumentMenuFormatter which uses FolderTreeBuilder to group items. DocumentMenu / DocumentMenuGroup / DocumentMenuItem public record DocumentMenu(IReadOnlyList<DocumentMenuGroup> Groups, bool UseSubmenus, Maybe<string> Title); public record DocumentMenuGroup(string DisplayName, IReadOnlyList<DocumentMenuItem> Items, ...); public record DocumentMenuItem(string DisplayName, IDocumentSource Source, MenuAction Action); Commands CreateDocument.Command (DocumentManagement/CreateDocument.cs): public class Command : ICommand { public string Value { get; } // Template name or path public string DisplayName { get; } } Handler flow: ITemplateRepository.GetByNameOrPath() - resolve template IDocumentManagementService.CreateDocument(template) - Word Documents.Add() Optional pre-profile via DMS configuration Dispatch DocumentCreatedFromTemplateEvent Entitlements: WordDocumentManagement + WordStandardTools CreateDocumentFromStarterDocument.Command (DocumentManagement/CreateDocumentFromStarterDocument.cs): public class Command(string starterDocumentPath) : ICommand Handler flow: DocumentFileFactory.Create(path) - validate path + existence IDocumentRepository.GetStarterDocument(documentFile) - inspect XML, resolve template IDocumentManagementService.CreateDocument(starterDocument) - Word Documents.Add() Optional pre-profile via DMS configuration Dispatch DocumentCreatedFromStarterDocument event Entitlements: WordDocumentManagement + WordStandardTools InsertSnippet.Command (Word/InsertSnippet.cs): public class Command(string sourcePath) : ICommand Handler flow: DocumentPath.Create(sourcePath) - validate path IDocumentEditor.InsertFile(documentPath) - Word Selection.InsertFile() Entitlements: WordStandardTools. Decorators: [DocumentRequired] Application Events DocumentCreatedFromTemplateEvent - raised after CreateDocument succeeds DocumentCreatedFromStarterDocument - raised after CreateDocumentFromStarterDocument succeeds Both implement IApplicationEvent and are dispatched asynchronously for side effects (DMS profiling, VBA callbacks). NB: Insert Snippet does not raise an application event. Infrastructure Layer FileProviderRepository (Infrastructure.Persistence) Implements IFileProviderRepository. Reads FileProviderConfiguration and delegates to FileDiscoveryService: public IReadOnlyList<IDocumentSource> GetDocuments(DocumentMenuType menuType, string id) Resolves path placeholders (%Workgroup%, %User%) via IEnvironmentService.GetPaths(). FileDiscoveryService Recursively walks the filesystem up to MaxDepth levels. Exclusions: Temporary lock files (~$ prefix) Non-matching extensions Non-matching prefixes (when configured) Directories in ExcludedContainers Reparse points (symlinks / junctions) FolderTreeBuilder Builds the group hierarchy from IDocumentSource lists: Finds common root path, strips it Compacts single-child directory chains: "Parent / Child" instead of nested menus Max depth: 2 levels (deeper items flattened into level 2) Items within a group sorted alphabetically Optional leading-number stripping from names DisplayNameFormatter Strips file extensions from display names Strips numeric prefixes (01_, 01-, 01., 01 , 01) unless the entire name is digits (e.g. locale code 1033) VSTO Layer (WordAddIn) ProductRibbon Callbacks Content callbacks: public string GetNewDocuments(IRibbonControl button) => GetDocumentMenu(DocumentMenuType.NewDocument, button.Id); public string GetStarterDocuments(IRibbonControl button) => GetDocumentMenu(DocumentMenuType.StarterDocument, button.Id); public string GetSnippetDocuments(IRibbonControl button) => GetDocumentMenu(DocumentMenuType.Snippet, button.Id); Visibility and enabled callbacks are handled by the Ribbon Item Rules subsystem. Dynamic menus typically use GetVisibleById / GetEnabledById attributes in Ribbon XML: <dynamicMenu id="newDocumentMenu" getVisible="GetVisibleById" getEnabled="GetEnabledById" getContent="GetNewDocuments" /> Action handlers: public void CreateDocument(IRibbonControl control) // dispatches CreateDocument.Command public void CreateFromStarterDocument(IRibbonControl control) // dispatches CreateDocumentFromStarterDocument.Command public void InsertSnippet(IRibbonControl control) // dispatches InsertSnippet.Command All action handlers resolve the file path from RibbonTagCache using control.Tag. DocumentMenuXmlRenderer Renders DocumentMenu to Office Ribbon XML. Per item: Resolves icon via IMenuItemIconResolver Composes screentip with last modified date: "TemplateName (dd/MM/yyyy)" Registers path in RibbonTagCache, embeds tag in tag= attribute For grouped menus: uses menu elements for submenus, menuSeparator for flat separators MenuItemIconResolver Resolves icons per menu item. For Starter Documents with staleness configuration, applies colour-coded icons based on file age thresholds. RibbonTagCache Thread-safe cache mapping opaque string keys to file paths. Prevents file paths from appearing in generated XML. Each Render() call registers new entries; entries are never removed during the application lifecycle. Configuration Format FileProviderConfiguration Located in the CoreConfiguration JSON file at the shared or user config path. { "FileProviderConfiguration": { "NewDocument": { }, "StarterDocument": { }, "InsertSnippet": { } } } Each section is a dictionary keyed by ribbon button id. This allows multiple ribbon buttons to show different file sets from the same menu type. FileProviderDto public class FileProviderDto { public LocalSourceDto Source { get; init; } public DisplayStyleDto? Style { get; init; } } LocalSourceDto public class LocalSourceDto { public string Location { get; init; } = "%Workgroup%"; public int? MaxDepth { get; init; } = 0; public IReadOnlyList<string> Extensions { get; init; } = []; public IReadOnlyList<string> Prefixes { get; init; } = []; public IReadOnlyList<string> ExcludedContainers { get; init; } = []; } DisplayStyleDto public class DisplayStyleDto { public string? Title { get; init; } public DisplayType Type { get; init; } = DisplayType.Flat; public bool RemoveLeadingNumbersFromTemplateNames { get; init; } = true; public bool RemoveLeadingNumbersFromContainerNames { get; init; } = true; public bool SeparateByContainer { get; init; } = true; public StalenessConfigDto? Staleness { get; init; } } DisplayType public enum DisplayType { Flat, Group } Flat: FlatMenuDisplayStyle - all items in one group, optional separator headers by container Group: GroupedMenuDisplayStyle - each container becomes a submenu (max 2 levels) Adding a New Menu Type Step 1: Add to DocumentMenuType enum Add a new value to DocumentMenuType in Core.Application/Document/DocumentMenuType.cs. Step 2: Create a MenuAction Add a static MenuAction in Core.Application/Document/MenuActions.cs with the ribbon callback name. Step 3: Add default extensions Add default extensions in Core.Application/Document/FileProviderDefaults.cs. Step 4: Add configuration section Add a new dictionary property to FileProviderConfiguration in Infrastructure.Persistence/Files/FileProviderConfiguration.cs. Step 5: Add ribbon callbacks In WordAddIn/Ribbon/ProductRibbon.cs: Add a GetContent callback that calls GetDocumentMenu() with the new type Add an onAction callback that dispatches the appropriate command Step 6: Create the command Create a new command + handler in Core.Application following the existing patterns (CreateDocument.cs or InsertSnippet.cs). Starter Document Template Resolution The starter document pipeline has an additional step: template resolution. When a user selects a starter document: DocumentFileFactory.Create(path) | v WordDocumentRepository.GetStarterDocument(documentFile) |-- InspectFile(documentSource) // Read XML from .docx |-- inspection.TemplateName // Attached template name/path |-- templateRepository.GetByNameOrPath(fullName) // Try full path |-- templateRepository.GetByNameOrPath(fileName) // Try just filename |-- templateRepository.GetDefault() // Fallback | v StarterDocument(source, resolvedTemplate) Design Rationale: Starter documents carry their template reference internally rather than via configuration. This means the same starter document always produces a document with the correct template, regardless of which menu or configuration triggered it. COM Safety All Word COM operations in the document creation pipeline are wrapped in try/catch with proper COM object disposal. Key patterns: Documents.Add() returns a Document COM object that must be tracked Pre-profile dialogs are synchronous COM calls; if cancelled, the created document may need to be closed Selection.InsertFile() (Insert Snippet) operates on the active selection and does not create new COM objects Testing Strategy Unit Tests Area Location Coverage InsertSnippet command InsertSnippet.feature Path validation, editor delegation GetDocumentMenuItems query GetSnippetDocumentMenuItems.feature Repository integration FormatDocumentMenu query FormatInsertSnippetMenu.feature, FormatNewDocumentMenu.feature, FormatStarterDocumentMenu.feature Menu action mapping, formatting FolderTreeBuilder FolderTreeBuilder.feature Grouping, compaction, depth limits DisplayNameFormatter DisplayNameFormatter.feature Extension stripping, number stripping BDD Scenarios All features use Reqnroll (SpecFlow successor) with Given/When/Then scenarios. COM Interface (External Automation) External VBA macros or DMS systems can trigger document creation programmatically: ' New Document from template comInterface.RunOperation("DMSNewDocument", "templateNameOrPath") ' New Document from Starter Document comInterface.RunOperation("DMSNewDocumentFromStarterDocument", "C:\StarterDocs\Agreement.docx") Both dispatch the same commands as the ribbon buttons and return the created Word Document COM object. References User Guide: New Document User Guide: Starter Document User Guide: Insert Snippet Developer Guide: Ribbon Item Rules

docs/ribbon-item-rules.html.title: Ribbon Item Rules - Developer Guide | Developer Docs
docs/ribbon-item-rules.html.summary: Ribbon Item Rules - Developer Guide Technical implementation guide for the rules-based system that controls ribbon item visibility and enabled state. This system runs in parallel with the existing script-based visibility (GetVisibleByScript/GetEnabledByScript) but targets non-script ribbon elements such as dynamic menus and groups. Architecture Overview Ribbon invalidated (startup, config change, document event) | v Office calls getVisible/getEnabled callback on each ribbon element | v ProductRibbon.GetVisibleById(control) / GetEnabledById(control) | v GetVisible.Query(control.Id) / GetEnabled.Query(control.Id) | v ItemRuleRepository.GetIsVisible(id) / GetIsEnabled(id) |-- Iterates RulesConfiguration.RibbonItemRules in order |-- First glob pattern match wins |-- "Always" or empty → true |-- Otherwise → delegates to IApplicationRulesEngine.IsVisible/IsEnabled(workflowName) | v Maybe<bool> → Handler applies default (visible=true, enabled=false) | v bool returned to Office Design Decisions Defaults: Visible defaults to true (items don't vanish), Enabled defaults to false (items are greyed-out until explicitly enabled). This prevents users from clicking unconfigured items. Glob patterns: Uses DotNet.Glob for pattern matching. Chosen over regex for simplicity and familiarity. First-match-wins: Rules are evaluated in declaration order. This allows specific overrides before catch-all patterns. Maybe return : The repository returns Maybe<bool>.None when no rule matches, allowing each handler to apply its own default. Layer Structure Application Layer (Core.Application.Rules) GetVisible.Query / GetEnabled.Query Thin query handlers that delegate to IItemRuleRepository: public static class GetVisible { public record Query(string Id) : IQuery; public class Handler(IItemRuleRepository repository) : IQueryHandler<Query, bool> { public Result<bool, Error> Handle(Query query) { var maybeVisible = repository.GetIsVisible(query.Id); return maybeVisible.GetValueOrDefault(true); // default: visible } } } public static class GetEnabled { public record Query(string Id) : IQuery; public class Handler(IItemRuleRepository repository) : IQueryHandler<Query, bool> { public Result<bool, Error> Handle(Query query) { var maybeEnabled = repository.GetIsEnabled(query.Id); return maybeEnabled.GetValueOrDefault(); // default: false (disabled) } } } RuleConstants public static class RuleConstants { public const string Always = "Always"; public static bool IsAlways(string value) => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) || value.Equals(Always, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase); } "Always", empty string, and null all resolve to unconditionally true — simplifies configuration. IItemRuleRepository public interface IItemRuleRepository { Maybe<bool> GetIsVisible(string id); Maybe<bool> GetIsEnabled(string id); } Returns Maybe<bool>.None when no rule matches, allowing the handler to apply its domain-specific default. Infrastructure Layer (Infrastructure.Persistence.Rules) RulesConfiguration public class RulesConfiguration { public IEnumerable<Workflow> Workflows { get; init; } = []; public IList<ItemMappingDto> RibbonItemRules { get; init; } = []; } Bound from RulesConfiguration JSON section via IOptionsMonitor<RulesConfiguration>. Hot-reloads on file change. ItemMappingDto public record ItemMappingDto { [Required] public string Pattern { get; init; } = string.Empty; public string EnabledWhen { get; init; } = RuleConstants.Always; public string VisibleWhen { get; init; } = RuleConstants.Always; } ItemRuleRepository Core matching logic: public class ItemRuleRepository( IOptionsMonitor<RulesConfiguration> ribbonItemRulesConfig, IApplicationRulesEngine rulesEngine) : IItemRuleRepository { private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, Glob> _globCache = new(); private Maybe<bool> Evaluate( string id, Func<ItemMappingDto, string> ruleSelector, Func<string, Maybe<bool>> engineEvaluator) { foreach (var rule in ribbonItemRulesConfig.CurrentValue.RibbonItemRules) { if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(rule.Pattern)) continue; if (rule.Pattern != "*" && !GetOrParseGlob(rule.Pattern).IsMatch(id)) continue; var whenValue = ruleSelector(rule); return RuleConstants.IsAlways(whenValue) ? true : engineEvaluator(whenValue); } return Maybe<bool>.None; } } Key behaviours: Glob cache: Parsed glob patterns are cached in a ConcurrentDictionary for performance — ribbon callbacks fire frequently during invalidation Wildcard shortcut: "*" pattern bypasses glob parsing (always matches) Empty pattern skip: Rules with blank patterns are silently ignored First match wins: Iteration stops at the first matching pattern VSTO Layer (WordAddIn.Ribbon) ProductRibbon Callbacks Four callbacks are available for use in Ribbon XML: public bool GetVisibleById(IRibbonControl control) => GetVisible(control.Id); public bool GetEnabledById(IRibbonControl control) => GetEnabled(control.Id); public bool GetVisibleByTag(IRibbonControl control) => GetVisible(control.Tag); public bool GetEnabledByTag(IRibbonControl control) => GetEnabled(control.Tag); The ById variants match against the element's id attribute; the ByTag variants match against tag. Both delegate to the same query pipeline. Private helpers apply safe defaults when the value is null/empty or when the query fails: private bool GetVisible(string value) { if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(value)) return true; var query = new GetVisible.Query(value); var result = _queryDispatcher.Dispatch<GetVisible.Query, bool>(query); return result.GetValueOrDefault(defaultValue: true); } private bool GetEnabled(string value) { if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(value)) return false; var query = new GetEnabled.Query(value); var result = _queryDispatcher.Dispatch<GetEnabled.Query, bool>(query); return result.GetValueOrDefault(defaultValue: false); } Ribbon Refresh RulesConfiguration is registered with IOptionsMonitor. On configuration file change: options.OnChange(_ => { Globals.ThisAddIn.UiSyncContext.Post(_ => Refresh(), null); }); Refresh() calls RibbonUi.Invalidate(), which triggers Office to re-query all getVisible/getEnabled callbacks. Configuration Format { "RulesConfiguration": { "Workflows": [ { "WorkflowName": "RibbonRules", "Rules": [ { "RuleName": "DocumentIsOpen", "Expression": "ActiveDocumentName != null" } ] } ], "RibbonItemRules": [ { "Pattern": "newDocumentMenu", "VisibleWhen": "Always", "EnabledWhen": "DocumentIsOpen" }, { "Pattern": "starter*", "VisibleWhen": "Always", "EnabledWhen": "DocumentIsOpen" }, { "Pattern": "*", "VisibleWhen": "Always", "EnabledWhen": "Always" } ] } } Relationship to Script Visibility The codebase has two parallel visibility/enabled systems: Script System Ribbon Item Rules Ribbon callbacks GetVisibleByScript / GetEnabledByScript GetVisibleById / GetEnabledById / ByTag Configuration ScriptConfiguration.Mappings[id].VisibleWhen RulesConfiguration.RibbonItemRules[].VisibleWhen Matching Exact id lookup in dictionary Glob pattern matching (first match) Repository ScriptRepository ItemRuleRepository Target elements Buttons with onAction="RunScript" Any ribbon element (menus, groups, buttons) Rules engine Same IApplicationRulesEngine Same IApplicationRulesEngine Both systems resolve workflow names through IApplicationRulesEngine, so the same workflow definitions work for both. Testing Strategy BDD Scenarios Feature Location Coverage GetVisible Features/Rules/GetVisible.feature Default visible, rule visible, rule hidden GetEnabled Features/Rules/GetEnabled.feature Default disabled, rule enabled, rule disabled Test Setup Step definitions use NSubstitute to mock IItemRuleRepository: Given("a visibility rule that evaluates to visible") => _repository.GetIsVisible(Arg.Any<string>()).Returns(Maybe<bool>.From(true)); Given("no visibility rule matches the item") => _repository.GetIsVisible(Arg.Any<string>()).Returns(Maybe<bool>.None); Adding a New Callback Variant To add a new matching strategy (e.g. matching by a custom ribbon attribute): Add a new public method in ProductRibbon.cs that extracts the value from IRibbonControl Delegate to the existing GetVisible(string) / GetEnabled(string) private methods Reference the new callback name in Ribbon XML No changes needed in the Application or Infrastructure layers. References User Guide: Ribbon Item Rules User Guide: Script Configuration Developer Guide: Document Menus

docs/scripts.html.title: Scripts - Developer Guide | Developer Docs
docs/scripts.html.summary: Scripts - Developer Guide Technical implementation guide for the scripting subsystem, covering architecture, domain model, configuration, and extension points. Architecture Overview The scripting subsystem implements a Command Executor pattern enabling users to define document formatting and editing scripts through JSON configuration. Scripts are composed of immutable command objects that are executed sequentially against Word documents via VSTO COM interop. Architectural Pattern Evolution The current architecture represents a significant refactoring from the original design (October 2025). See ADR: Refactor Scripting to Command Executor Pattern for the full decision context. Previous Architecture (deprecated): IScriptCommand.Execute() → IScriptExecutor → DocumentScriptExecutor (adapter) → IDocumentEditor → Word COM Current Architecture (dispatcher pattern with individual handlers): IScriptCommand (pure data) → IScriptCommandDispatcher.Execute() → Dynamic dispatch → IScriptCommandHandler<T> → Word COM Key Architectural Principles Pure Data Commands: IScriptCommand implementations are immutable records with no behaviour Dispatcher Pattern: IScriptCommandDispatcher routes commands to appropriate handlers via dynamic dispatch Individual Handlers: Each command type has its own IScriptCommandHandler<T> implementation Backend Agnostic: Design supports multiple backends (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, PDF) CQRS Integration: Scripts execute via CQRS handlers in the application layer Clean Architecture: Domain layer has no infrastructure dependencies Open/Closed Principle: New commands can be added without modifying the dispatcher Layer Structure Domain Layer (Core.Domain.Scripting) Pure domain logic with no external dependencies. Script Aggregate Root public class Script : AggregateRoot<string> { public string Name { get; } public string Description { get; } public IReadOnlyCollection<IScriptCommand> Commands { get; } } The Script aggregate is the root entity representing a collection of commands to execute. The Name serves as the aggregate ID. Invariants: Name must be unique within the repository Commands collection is immutable after construction Command order is preserved IScriptCommand Interface Marker interface for all script commands: public interface IScriptCommand { } All command implementations are immutable records representing pure data: public record ApplyFontFormatScriptCommand( FontStyle Style, FontFormattingRange Range) : IScriptCommand; public record DeleteCharactersScriptCommand( string Characters, CommandRange Range, CommandSubRange SubRange) : IScriptCommand; public record ApplyStyleScriptCommand( StyleFormatting Style, FormattingRange Range) : IScriptCommand; Design Rationale: Commands contain only data (what to do), whilst executors contain behaviour (how to do it). This separation enables: Serialisation for audit logging Batching for performance optimisation Transformation and composition Backend-agnostic command representation Available Commands Command Type Purpose Domain Model Location ApplyFontFormatScriptCommand Character formatting Fonts/ApplyFontFormatScriptCommand.cs ApplyParagraphFormatScriptCommand Paragraph formatting Paragraphs/ApplyParagraphFormatScriptCommand.cs ApplyStyleScriptCommand Style application Styles/ApplyStyleScriptCommand.cs ApplySelectionFormatScriptCommand Combined formatting Selection/SelectionFormattingCommand.cs InsertTextScriptCommand Text insertion (with optional cursor repositioning) Insertion/Text/InsertTextScriptCommand.cs InsertFieldScriptCommand Field insertion Insertion/Field/InsertFieldScriptCommand.cs DeleteCharactersScriptCommand Character deletion Deletion/DeleteCharactersScriptCommand.cs EnforceCaseScriptCommand Case conversion EnforceCasing/EnforceCaseScriptCommand.cs SetCustomPropertyScriptCommand Set document property Properties/SetCustomPropertyScriptCommand.cs DeleteCustomPropertyScriptCommand Delete document property Properties/DeleteCustomPropertyScriptCommand.cs RunMacroScriptCommand Execute VBA macros Macro/RunMacroScriptCommand.cs IScriptRepository Repository interface for script persistence: public interface IScriptRepository { IEnumerable<Script> GetScripts(); Maybe<Script> GetScript(string name); Maybe<bool> IsEnabled(string mappingId); Maybe<bool> IsVisible(string mappingId); } Separates script data (scripts collection) from UI metadata (mappings). Application Layer (Core.Application.Scripting) CQRS handlers orchestrating script operations. Query Handlers GetScripts: Retrieves all available scripts public class Handler(IScriptRepository repository) : IQueryHandler<Query, Result<IEnumerable<Script>>> { public Result<IEnumerable<Script>> Handle(Query query) => Result.Success(repository.GetScripts()); } GetScriptEnabled: Checks if a script is enabled public Result<Maybe<bool>> Handle(Query query) { var enabled = repository.IsEnabled(query.MappingId); return Result.Success(enabled); } GetScriptVisible: Checks if a script is visible in UI public Result<Maybe<bool>> Handle(Query query) { var visible = repository.IsVisible(query.MappingId); return Result.Success(visible); } Command Handler RunScript: Executes a script's commands sequentially public class Handler( IScriptRepository repository, IScriptCommandDispatcher dispatcher, ILogger<Handler> logger) : ICommandHandler<Command, Result> { public Result Handle(Command command) { var scriptResult = repository.GetScript(command.ScriptName); if (scriptResult.HasNoValue) return Result.Failure($"Script {command.ScriptName} not found"); var script = scriptResult.Value; foreach (var scriptCommand in script.Commands) { var result = dispatcher.Execute(scriptCommand); if (result.IsFailure) { logger.LogError("Command failed: {Error}", result.Error); return result; } } return Result.Success(); } } Key Behaviours: Sequential execution - stops on first failure Logging of command failures Transaction-like semantics (all or nothing) Cancellation token support (via extension) IScriptCommandDispatcher Interface Dispatcher interface for routing commands to handlers: public interface IScriptCommandDispatcher { Result Execute(IScriptCommand command); } Design Note: This interface abstracts the backend implementation (Word, Excel, etc.) from the application layer. The dispatcher uses runtime type resolution to invoke the correct IScriptCommandHandler<T> for each command type. IScriptCommandHandler Interface Generic handler interface for individual command types: public interface IScriptCommandHandler<in T> where T : IScriptCommand { Result Handle(T command); } Enables: Individual command handlers per type Dependency injection per handler Programmatic reuse of script commands Open/Closed Principle compliance Independent testing of each command type Infrastructure Layer Persistence (Core.Infrastructure.Persistence) ScriptRepository: Loads scripts from JSON configuration public class ScriptRepository( IOptionsMonitor<ScriptConfiguration> configuration, ILogger<ScriptRepository> logger) : IScriptRepository { public IEnumerable<Script> GetScripts() { var config = configuration.CurrentValue; return config.Scripts.Select(kvp => MapToScript(kvp.Key, kvp.Value)); } } Key Features: Hot-reloading via IOptionsMonitor<T> Bidirectional mapping (DTO ↔ Domain) JSON polymorphism for command types Configuration DTOs ScriptDto: Represents a script in JSON public class ScriptDto { public string Comment { get; set; } public IList<ScriptCommandDto> Commands { get; init; } } ScriptConfiguration: Root configuration object public class ScriptConfiguration { public IDictionary<string, ScriptDto> Scripts { get; init; } public IDictionary<string, ScriptMapping> Mappings { get; init; } } ScriptMapping: Controls UI behaviour public class ScriptMapping { public string Script { get; init; } // References a script name public string EnabledWhen { get; init; } // Rules expression public string VisibleWhen { get; init; } // Rules expression } Design Rationale: Separation of scripts (what) from mappings (when/where) enables: Multiple UI buttons for same script Context-dependent visibility/enablement Rules engine integration Command DTOs Base class using JSON polymorphism: [JsonPolymorphic] [JsonDerivedType(typeof(ApplyFontFormattingScriptCommandDto), "ApplyFontFormatting")] [JsonDerivedType(typeof(DeleteCharactersScriptCommandDto), "DeleteCharacters")] // ... other command types public abstract class ScriptCommandDto { [Required] public CommandRange CommandRange { get; set; } } Example concrete DTO: public class DeleteCharactersScriptCommandDto : ScriptCommandDto, IHasSubRange { [JsonRequired] public string Characters { get; set; } [JsonRequired] public CommandSubRange SubRange { get; set; } } DTO Mapping: ScriptRepository handles bidirectional mapping between DTOs and domain commands. Common Infrastructure (Core.Infrastructure.Common) ScriptCommandDispatcher: Dynamic dispatch implementation Located in Core.Infrastructure.Common.Messaging, this dispatcher uses runtime type resolution to route commands to their registered handlers: public class ScriptCommandDispatcher( IServiceProvider serviceProvider, ILogger<ScriptCommandDispatcher> logger) : IScriptCommandDispatcher { public Result Execute(IScriptCommand command) { if (command is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(command)); try { // Resolve handler type at runtime based on command's actual type var commandType = command.GetType(); var handlerType = typeof(IScriptCommandHandler<>).MakeGenericType(commandType); logger.LogDebug("Resolving handler {HandlerType} for command {CommandType}", handlerType.Name, commandType.Name); // Try to get handler from DI var handler = serviceProvider.GetService(handlerType); if (handler is null) { logger.LogWarning("No handler registered for script command type {CommandType}", commandType.Name); return Result.Failure($"No handler registered for {commandType.Name}"); } // Dynamic dispatch: CLR resolves to correct Handle(TCommand) method at runtime return ((dynamic)handler).Handle((dynamic)command); } catch (Exception ex) { logger.LogError(ex, "Error dispatching script command {CommandType}", command.GetType().Name); return Result.Failure($"Error dispatching command: {ex.Message}"); } } } Key Features: Generic type resolution at runtime Dynamic dispatch to strongly-typed handlers Automatic handler discovery via dependency injection No modification required when adding new command types VSTO Command Handlers (WordAddIn.Features.Scripting.Handlers) All script commands are implemented as individual handlers. Example handler: public class DeleteCharactersScriptCommandHandler( ITextReplacer textReplacer, ILogger<DeleteCharactersScriptCommandHandler> logger) : IScriptCommandHandler<DeleteCharactersScriptCommand> { private readonly Application _wordApp = Globals.ThisAddIn.Application; public Result Handle(DeleteCharactersScriptCommand command) { try { _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = false; _wordApp.System.Cursor = WdCursorType.wdCursorWait; // Implementation using Word COM interop var result = textReplacer.Replace( command.Characters, string.Empty, command.Range); return result; } catch (COMException ex) { logger.LogError(ex, "COM error deleting characters"); return Result.Failure($"Error: {ex.Message}"); } finally { _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = true; _wordApp.System.Cursor = WdCursorType.wdCursorNormal; } } } All Command Handlers (located in src/VSTO/WordAddIn/Features/Scripting/Handlers): ApplyFontFormatScriptCommandHandler ApplyParagraphFormatScriptCommandHandler ApplyStyleScriptCommandHandler ApplySelectionFormatScriptCommandHandler InsertTextScriptCommandHandler InsertFieldScriptCommandHandler DeleteCharactersScriptCommandHandler EnforceCaseScriptCommandHandler SetCustomPropertyScriptCommandHandler DeleteCustomPropertyScriptCommandHandler Handler Pattern Benefits: Independent testability Focused responsibilities Programmatic reuse via DI No modification of dispatcher for new commands Clean separation of concerns Configuration Format File Location Scripts are loaded from JSON files via .NET Configuration system: Shared: %appdata%/kutana+brochet/Word/Config/scripts.json User: %localappdata%/kutana+brochet/Word/Config/scripts.json Configuration locations are defined in appsettings.json: { "ConfigurationLocations": { "Shared": "%appdata%/kutana+brochet/Word/Config", "User": "%localappdata%/kutana+brochet/Word/Config" } } JSON Schema The JSON Schema is auto-generated from the C# DTOs using SchemaGenerator. See CoreConfiguration.schema.json for the full schema. Schema Generation: Run SchemaGenerator project to regenerate schemas when DTOs change. The schema provides: IntelliSense in JSON editors Compile-time validation of configuration files Documentation of available properties and their types Enum value validation Complete Configuration Example { "Scripts": { "FormatLegalDocument": { "Comment": "Applies standard legal document formatting", "Commands": [ { "$type": "ApplyStyle", "CommandRange": "AllParagraphs", "StyleName": "Legal Body Text" }, { "$type": "ApplyFontFormatting", "CommandRange": "Selection", "SubRange": "All", "Bold": 0, "Italic": 0, "Colour": "RGB(0,0,0)" }, { "$type": "DeleteCharacters", "CommandRange": "EntireDocument", "SubRange": "All", "Characters": " " } ] }, "RunLegacyMacro": { "Comment": "Executes a VBA macro from the attached template", "Commands": [ { "$type": "RunMacro", "Options": { "MacroName": "CustomModule.FormatDocument", "Parameters": ["Standard", true] } } ] } }, "Mappings": { "formatLegalDoc": { "Script": "FormatLegalDocument", "EnabledWhen": "Always", "VisibleWhen": "HasEntitlement('WordStandardTools')" }, "runMacro": { "Script": "RunLegacyMacro", "EnabledWhen": "Always", "VisibleWhen": "Always" } } } CommandRange Enumeration public enum CommandRange { Selection, EntireDocument, FirstParagraph, LastParagraph, AllParagraphs } CommandSubRange Enumeration public enum CommandSubRange { All, FirstWord, LastWord, AllWords, FirstSentence, LastSentence, AllSentences } AfterInsertionBehavior Enumeration Controls cursor placement after an InsertText command completes. Located in Core.Application.Scripting.Commands.Insertion. public enum AfterInsertionBehavior { None, // No cursor movement (default) MoveCursorToEnd, // Collapse cursor to end of inserted/existing text MoveCursorToStart, // Collapse cursor to start of inserted/existing text SelectInsertedText // Select the full span of inserted/existing text } Design Notes: The InsertTextOptions.AfterInsertion property defaults to None for backward compatibility Cursor repositioning applies to both freshly inserted text and duplicate-skipped text (when PreventDuplicates is true) When multiple ranges are processed, the cursor is positioned relative to the last range Implementation tracks range boundaries through both insertion and duplicate-skip paths, then applies the cursor behaviour in a single pass after all ranges are processed Adding New Commands Step 1: Create Domain Command File: src/Core/Core.Domain/Scripting/{Category}/{CommandName}ScriptCommand.cs namespace KutanaBrochet.Core.Domain.Scripting.{Category}; public record {CommandName}ScriptCommand( {Type} {Property}, CommandRange Range, CommandSubRange SubRange) : IScriptCommand; Step 2: Create DTO File: src/Core/Infrastructure.Persistence/Configuration/Commands/{CommandName}ScriptCommandDto.cs public class {CommandName}ScriptCommandDto : ScriptCommandDto, IHasSubRange { [JsonRequired] public {Type} {Property} { get; set; } [JsonRequired] public CommandSubRange SubRange { get; set; } } Update ScriptCommand.cs to register the JSON type discriminator: [JsonDerivedType(typeof({CommandName}ScriptCommand), "{CommandName}")] Note: For commands without Range (like RunMacroScriptCommand, SetCustomPropertyScriptCommand), inherit directly from ScriptCommand and implement Execute() to delegate to IDocumentEditor. Step 3: Create Handler File: src/VSTO/WordAddIn/Features/Scripting/Handlers/{CommandName}Handler.cs public class {CommandName}Handler( {IDependency} dependency, ILogger<{CommandName}Handler> logger) : IScriptCommandHandler<{CommandName}ScriptCommand> { private readonly Application _wordApp = Globals.ThisAddIn.Application; public Result Handle({CommandName}ScriptCommand command) { try { _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = false; _wordApp.System.Cursor = WdCursorType.wdCursorWait; // Implementation return Result.Success(); } catch (COMException ex) { logger.LogError(ex, "COM error executing {CommandName}"); return Result.Failure($"Error: {ex.Message}"); } finally { _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = true; _wordApp.System.Cursor = WdCursorType.wdCursorNormal; } } } Step 4: Handler Auto-Registration Handlers are automatically registered via Scrutor in ThisAddIn.cs: builder.Services.Scan(s => s.FromAssemblyOf<WordScriptCommandHandlerBase>() .AddClasses(c => c.AssignableTo(typeof(IScriptCommandHandler<>)), publicOnly: false) .AsImplementedInterfaces() .WithTransientLifetime()); No manual registration required: Scrutor automatically discovers all classes implementing IScriptCommandHandler<T> Handlers are registered with transient lifetime The dispatcher will automatically find and invoke your new handler Step 5: Add DTO Mapping File: src/Core/Infrastructure.Persistence/ScriptRepository.cs Add mapping logic in MapToScript() method: private Script MapToScript(string name, ScriptDto dto) { var commands = dto.Commands.Select(cmd => cmd switch { {CommandName}ScriptCommandDto c => new {CommandName}ScriptCommand( c.{Property}, c.CommandRange, c.SubRange), // ... other command types }); return new Script(name, dto.Comment, commands.ToList()); } Step 6: Write BDD Tests Feature File: tests/Core.Application.Tests/Features/Scripts/{CommandName}.feature Feature: {Command Name} As a document author I want to {capability} So that I can {benefit} Scenario: {Scenario name} Given {precondition} When I execute the {command name} command Then the result should be successful And {expected outcome} Step Definitions: Implement the Given/When/Then steps. Testing Strategy Unit Tests Domain Tests: Verify script aggregate invariants Script name uniqueness Command order preservation Immutability of commands collection Application Tests: BDD scenarios for each command Command execution logic Error handling Integration with repository Infrastructure Tests: Configuration loading JSON deserialisation DTO to domain mapping Configuration hot-reloading Integration Tests VSTO Tests: Word COM interop Command execution against real Word documents COM exception handling Resource cleanup (Marshal.ReleaseComObject) Test Structure tests/ ├── Core.Application.Tests/ │ └── Features/ │ └── Scripts/ │ ├── GetScripts.feature │ ├── RunScript.feature │ ├── ApplyFontFormatting.feature │ └── ... ├── Core.Domain.Tests/ │ └── Scripting/ │ └── ScriptTests.cs └── WordAddIn.Tests/ └── Features/ └── Scripting/ └── WordScriptCommandExecutor.feature COM Safety and Error Handling Critical VSTO Practices 1. COM Exception Handling Always wrap Word API calls: try { // Word COM operations } catch (COMException ex) { logger.LogError(ex, "COM error"); return Result.Failure($"Error: {ex.Message}"); } 2. COM Object Cleanup Release COM objects explicitly: Sentences sentences = null; try { sentences = range.Sentences; // Use sentences } finally { if (sentences != null) Marshal.ReleaseComObject(sentences); } 3. Screen Updating and Cursor Management try { _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = false; _wordApp.System.Cursor = WdCursorType.wdCursorWait; // Operations } finally { _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = true; _wordApp.System.Cursor = WdCursorType.wdCursorNormal; } 4. Infinite Loop Prevention int iterations = 0; const int maxIterations = 10000; while (condition && iterations < maxIterations) { // Operation iterations++; } if (iterations >= maxIterations) logger.LogWarning("Maximum iterations reached"); Performance Considerations Optimisation Strategies 1. Batch Operations Process multiple paragraphs/words/sentences in a single operation rather than one-by-one. 2. Minimise Range Access Cache Range objects rather than repeatedly accessing Selection, Paragraphs, etc. 3. Screen Updating Disable during bulk operations: _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = false; try { /* operations */ } finally { _wordApp.ScreenUpdating = true; } 4. Command Ordering Apply styles before character formatting Combine formatting commands when possible Use ApplySelectionFormatting for multiple format changes Known Bottlenecks Paragraph iteration: O(n) for each paragraph access Word/Sentence enumeration: Expensive for large documents Find/Replace operations: Can be slow on entire document Extension Points Adding Backend Support To support Excel/PowerPoint/PDF: 1. Create Backend-Specific Handlers // Example: Excel handler for applying font formatting public class ExcelApplyFontFormatScriptCommandHandler( ILogger<ExcelApplyFontFormatScriptCommandHandler> logger) : IScriptCommandHandler<ApplyFontFormatScriptCommand> { private readonly Excel.Application _excelApp = Globals.ThisAddIn.Application; public Result Handle(ApplyFontFormatScriptCommand command) { // Excel-specific implementation } } 2. Handlers Auto-Register via Scrutor Set up Scrutor in your Excel/PowerPoint add-in's ThisAddIn.cs: builder.Services.Scan(s => s.FromExecutingAssembly() .AddClasses(c => c.AssignableTo(typeof(IScriptCommandHandler<>))) .AsImplementedInterfaces() .WithTransientLifetime()); 3. Dispatcher Works Automatically The same ScriptCommandDispatcher from Core.Infrastructure.Common works across all backends: Dispatcher routes commands to backend-specific handlers No changes needed to domain commands Backend handlers can share interfaces/base classes for common logic Custom Command Types For organisation-specific commands: 1. Extend IScriptCommand public record CustomFormatCommand( string CustomProperty, CommandRange Range) : IScriptCommand; 2. Create Handler public class CustomFormatHandler : IScriptCommandHandler<CustomFormatCommand> { public Result Handle(CustomFormatCommand command) { /* */ } } 3. Register JSON Type [JsonDerivedType(typeof(CustomFormatCommandDto), "CustomFormat")] Architecture Evolution The scripting subsystem has undergone two major refactorings to reach its current state: Phase 1 (October 2025): Refactored from the original multi-layered architecture to a Command Executor pattern with IScriptCommandExecutor using switch expression pattern matching. See ADR: Refactor Scripting to Command Executor Pattern. Phase 2 (October 2025): Migrated from monolithic executor to dispatcher pattern with individual handlers: ✅ Replaced IScriptCommandExecutor with IScriptCommandDispatcher ✅ Migrated all 8 command types to individual IScriptCommandHandler<T> implementations ✅ Removed switch expression entirely ✅ Implemented dynamic dispatch via runtime type resolution ✅ Auto-registration of handlers via Scrutor Result: The subsystem now follows the Open/Closed Principle - new commands can be added without modifying the dispatcher, and each command handler is independently testable and maintainable. References ADR: Refactor Scripting to Command Executor Pattern Script Command Migration Guide User Guide - End-user documentation Interpreter Pattern - Design pattern reference VSTO Programming Guide - Microsoft documentation

docs/square-brackets.html.title: Square Brackets - Developer Guide | Developer Docs
docs/square-brackets.html.summary: Square Brackets - Developer Guide Technical implementation guide for the Square Brackets feature. This feature provides tools for working with bracket pairs [...] in legal documents through navigation, addition, and deletion commands. Architecture Overview The Square Brackets feature follows Clean Architecture patterns with clear separation of concerns: WordAddIn (Presentation) └─> ProductRibbon.cs - Ribbon button handlers Core.Application (Use Cases) └─> Document/SquareBrackets/ ├─> FindSquareBrackets.cs - Navigation command ├─> AddSquareBrackets.cs - Add brackets command ├─> DeleteSquareBrackets.cs - Delete commands ├─> InsertBlob.cs - Insert blob command └─> Blob.cs - Blob value object Core.Domain (Entities) └─> Documents/ └─> Delimiter.cs - Delimiter value object Infrastructure.Persistence (Data) └─> Word/ ├─> SquareBracketsConfiguration.cs - Config model └─> SquareBracketsSettingsRepository.cs - Config access Key Architectural Principles CQRS Pattern: All operations are commands dispatched through ICommandDispatcher Strategy Pattern: Search and modification behaviors use pluggable strategy objects Value Objects: Delimiter and Blob are immutable value objects with factory methods Railway-Oriented Programming: All commands return Result<TValue, Error> Clean Architecture: Domain has no infrastructure dependencies; COM interop isolated in VSTO layer Layer Structure Domain Layer (Core.Domain.Documents) Delimiter Immutable value object representing opening and closing delimiter characters. public record Delimiter(string Open, string Close) { public static Delimiter Default() => new("[", "]"); } Application Layer (Core.Application) FindSquareBrackets Command Locates and selects square bracket pairs using the Strategy pattern for directional search. Command Definition: public class Command(bool searchForward) : ICommand { public bool SearchForward { get; } = searchForward; } Handler Flow: Get current selection from IDocumentEditor Select appropriate search strategy (ForwardSearchStrategy or BackwardSearchStrategy) Execute search via IDelimiterPairFinder If found: select the bracket pair If not found: notify user or prompt to restart from beginning/end Search Strategies: ForwardSearchStrategy - Searches from cursor position toward end of document BackwardSearchStrategy - Searches from cursor position toward start of document Returns: Result.Success<Unit, Error> - Bracket pair found and selected Result.Success<Unit, Error> - No brackets exist (user notified) Result.Success<Unit, Error> - End of document reached, restart prompt shown Result.Failure<Unit, Error> - Infrastructure error occurred Special Handling: Placeholder content controls showing brackets are treated as bracket pairs Allows consistent navigation across both text brackets and content control placeholders AddSquareBrackets Command Wraps the current selection in square brackets using AddDelimiterPairStrategy. Command Definition: public class Command(Delimiter delimiter) : ICommand { public Delimiter Delimiter { get; } = delimiter; public static Command Default => new(Delimiter.Default()); } Handler Flow: Get delimiter configuration from ISquareBracketsSettingsRepository Execute AddDelimiterPairStrategy via IDocumentModifier Expand selection to include new brackets Behaviour: Inserts opening delimiter before selection start Inserts closing delimiter after selection end Expands selection to include new brackets Works with empty selection (inserts [] at cursor) Creates nested brackets if text already contains brackets Returns: Result.Success<Unit, Error> - Brackets added successfully Result.Failure<Unit, Error> - Document modification failed Entitlements: WordStandardTools Decorators: [DocumentRequired] DeleteSquareBrackets Command Removes brackets and/or content using configurable deletion strategies. Command Definition: public class Command(DeletionMode mode, Delimiter delimiter) : ICommand { public DeletionMode Mode { get; } = mode; public Delimiter Delimiter { get; } = delimiter; public static Command DeleteDelimiterPair => new(DeletionMode.DeleteDelimiterPair, Delimiter.Default()); public static Command DeleteAll => new(DeletionMode.DeleteAll, Delimiter.Default()); } Handler Flow: Validate selection starts with [ and ends with ] If invalid: notify user and return success (no-op) Select appropriate deletion strategy based on mode Execute strategy via IDocumentModifier Deletion Strategies: DeleteDelimiterPair - Removes only [ and ], keeps content DeleteContent - Removes content, keeps [] DeleteAll - Removes everything including brackets Validation: Selection must start with opening delimiter Selection must end with closing delimiter Invalid selections show user notification, document unchanged Returns: Result.Success<Unit, Error> - Deletion completed Result.Success<Unit, Error> - Invalid selection (user notified) Result.Failure<Unit, Error> - Document modification failed Entitlements: WordStandardTools Decorators: [DocumentRequired] InsertBlob Command Replaces the current selection with a formatted marker between delimiters. Command Definition: public class Command(Delimiter delimiter, Blob blob) : ICommand { public Delimiter Delimiter { get; } = delimiter; public Blob Blob { get; } = blob; public static Command Default => new(Delimiter.Default(), Blob.Default()); } Handler Flow: Read blob configuration from ISquareBracketsSettingsRepository Build replacement text: [{blob.Text}] Replace selection via IDocumentModifier.ReplaceText() Apply font formatting to blob text only (not delimiters) Behaviour: Reads blob configuration from SquareBracketsConfiguration Replaces selection with [{blob text}] Applies font formatting to blob text only (not delimiters) Uses Wingdings font when blob text is empty Returns: Result.Success<Unit, Error> - Blob inserted successfully Result.Failure<Unit, Error> - Document modification failed Entitlements: WordStandardTools Decorators: [DocumentRequired] Blob Value Object Immutable value object representing blob insertion configuration. public record Blob(string Text, string? FontName, double? FontSize) { public static Blob Default() => new(string.Empty, "Wingdings", 10); } Infrastructure Layer (Infrastructure.Persistence.Word) SquareBracketsConfiguration Configuration model for Square Brackets operations. public class SquareBracketsConfiguration { public Delimiter Delimiter { get; set; } = Delimiter.Default; public Blob? Blob { get; set; } } SquareBracketsSettingsRepository Implements ISquareBracketsSettingsRepository to load configuration from JSON. public interface ISquareBracketsSettingsRepository { Delimiter GetDelimiter(); Maybe<Blob> GetBlob(); } Implementation reads from SquareBracketsConfiguration section with fallback to defaults: GetDelimiter() returns Delimiter.Default() if not configured GetBlob() returns Maybe.None if not configured, allowing caller to use Blob.Default() VSTO Layer (WordAddIn) ProductRibbon Callbacks Ribbon button handlers that dispatch commands: public void FindNextSquareBrackets(IRibbonControl _) { var command = new FindSquareBrackets.Command(searchForward: true); _commandDispatcher.Dispatch<FindSquareBrackets.Command, Unit>(command); } public void FindPreviousSquareBrackets(IRibbonControl _) { var command = new FindSquareBrackets.Command(searchForward: false); _commandDispatcher.Dispatch<FindSquareBrackets.Command, Unit>(command); } public void AddSquareBrackets(IRibbonControl _) { var command = Core.Application.Document.SquareBrackets.AddSquareBrackets.Default; _commandDispatcher.Dispatch<AddSquareBrackets.Command, Unit>(command); } public void DeleteSquareBrackets(IRibbonControl _) { var command = Core.Application.Document.SquareBrackets.DeleteSquareBrackets.DeleteDelimiterPair; _commandDispatcher.Dispatch<DeleteSquareBrackets.Command, Unit>(command); } public void DeleteSquareBracketsIncludingContent(IRibbonControl _) { var command = Core.Application.Document.SquareBrackets.DeleteSquareBrackets.DeleteAll; _commandDispatcher.Dispatch<DeleteSquareBrackets.Command, Unit>(command); } public void InsertBlob(IRibbonControl _) { var command = Core.Application.Document.SquareBrackets.InsertBlob.Default; _commandDispatcher.Dispatch<InsertBlob.Command, Unit>(command); } Visibility and enabled callbacks are handled by the Ribbon Item Rules subsystem. Strategy Pattern Implementation Square Brackets commands use the Strategy pattern to support multiple operational modes. Search Strategies Interface: public interface ISearchStrategy { Maybe<Range> Execute(Range startRange, Delimiter delimiter); } Implementations: ForwardSearchStrategy - Searches from cursor toward document end BackwardSearchStrategy - Searches from cursor toward document start Both strategies use Word's Range.Find API for optimal performance. Modification Strategies Interface: public interface IDelimiterPairStrategy { Result<Unit, Error> Execute(Range selection, Delimiter delimiter); } Implementations: AddDelimiterPairStrategy - Inserts delimiters around selection RemoveDelimiterPairStrategy - Removes delimiters, preserves content RemoveContentStrategy - Removes content, preserves delimiters See Strategy Pattern Guide for detailed implementation patterns. Configuration Format Configuration is located in the CoreConfiguration JSON file: { "$schema": "https://kutana-brochet.github.io/schemas/7.0/CoreConfiguration.schema.json", "SquareBracketsConfiguration": { "Delimiter": { "Open": "[", "Close": "]" }, "Blob": { "Text": "X", "FontName": "Wingdings", "FontSize": 10 } } } Delimiter Properties Property Type Default Description Open string [ Opening delimiter character Close string ] Closing delimiter character Blob Properties Property Type Default Description Text string Empty Text to insert between brackets FontName string Wingdings Font name for blob character FontSize double 10 Font size for blob character Testing Strategy Square Brackets functionality is tested using Reqnroll (BDD) scenarios. Feature Files Feature Location Scenarios Coverage FindSquareBrackets FindSquareBrackets.feature 11 Navigation, edge cases, content controls AddSquareBrackets AddSquareBrackets.feature 14 Addition, nesting, empty selection DeleteSquareBrackets DeleteSquareBrackets.feature 18 Deletion variants, validation InsertBlob InsertBlob.feature 3 Blob insertion, formatting Integration tests verify end-to-end behaviour with Word interop layer. Running Tests # Run all Square Brackets tests dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SquareBrackets" # Run specific feature dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~FindSquareBrackets" Test Patterns Step definitions use: IDocumentEditor for document manipulation IDocumentModifier for text modification NSubstitute for mocking infrastructure dependencies Example: [Given(@"my document contains Square Bracket Pairs")] public void GivenDocumentContainsSquareBracketPairs() { _documentEditor.InsertText("[First] and [Second]"); } [When(@"I run the Find Next command")] public void WhenIRunFindNextCommand() { var command = new FindSquareBrackets.Command(searchForward: true); _result = _handler.Handle(command); } [Then(@"the result is successful")] public void ThenResultIsSuccessful() { Assert.True(_result.IsSuccess); } Extension Points Custom Delimiters Override default delimiters via configuration to support different bracket types: { "SquareBracketsConfiguration": { "Delimiter": { "Open": "{", "Close": "}" } } } Important: Custom delimiters affect all Square Brackets operations. Ensure your choice doesn't conflict with other document syntax (e.g., mail merge fields use {{ }}). Custom Blob Markers Configure blob appearance for organizational standards: { "SquareBracketsConfiguration": { "Blob": { "Text": "●", "FontName": "Arial", "FontSize": 12 } } } Common patterns: Wingdings checkbox: Text: empty string, FontName: "Wingdings" X marker: Text: "X", FontName: "Arial" Bullet: Text: "●", FontName: "Arial" Ribbon Visibility Rules Control when Square Brackets buttons appear using Ribbon Item Rules: <button id="AddSquareBrackets" onAction="AddSquareBrackets" getVisible="GetVisibleById" getEnabled="GetEnabledById" label="Add Brackets" /> Configuration: { "RulesConfiguration": { "RibbonItemRules": { "AddSquareBrackets": { "VisibleWhen": "DocumentTemplate:Contract*", "EnabledWhen": "HasSelection" } } } } Performance Considerations COM Object Disposal All Word interop objects are released in finally blocks to prevent memory leaks: Word.Range range = null; try { range = document.Range(); // Operations } finally { if (range != null) Marshal.ReleaseComObject(range); } This pattern appears 73 times in the codebase and is critical for VSTO stability. Error Handling All commands return Result<TValue, Error> following railway-oriented programming: var result = _commandDispatcher.Dispatch<FindSquareBrackets.Command, Unit>(command); if (result.IsFailure) { _userNotifier.Notify(result.Error); } Common Error Codes Defined in DomainErrors.SquareBrackets: Error Code Meaning User Message NotFound No brackets in document "No square brackets found in document." InvalidSelection Selection doesn't match bracket pair "Selection must start with '[' and end with ']'." EndOfDocument Reached document boundary "End of document reached. Restart from beginning?" Infrastructure errors: Error Code Meaning Document.ModificationFailed Word interop error Document.NoActiveDocument No document open Localisation User-facing messages are localised via ILocalisationService. Message Resources (DomainMessages.resx): <data name="SquareBrackets.NotFound"> <value>No square brackets found in document.</value> </data> <data name="SquareBrackets.InvalidSelection"> <value>Selection must start with '[' and end with ']'.</value> </data> <data name="SquareBrackets.EndOfDocument"> <value>End of document reached. Restart from beginning?</value> </data> Adding Translations: Create DomainMessages.{locale}.resx files: DomainMessages.fr-FR.resx - French DomainMessages.de-DE.resx - German etc. Messages are resolved automatically based on Thread.CurrentThread.CurrentUICulture. COM Safety Patterns Document Validation Always check for active document before operations: if (Application.Documents.Count == 0) { return Result.Failure<Unit, Error>(DomainErrors.Document.NoActiveDocument); } Exception Handling Catch COM-specific exceptions: try { // Word operations } catch (System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException ex) { _logger.LogError(ex, "COM error in Square Brackets operation"); return Result.Failure<Unit, Error>(DomainErrors.Document.ModificationFailed); } Safe Word API Calls Validate COM objects before use: if (selection is null || !Marshal.IsComObject(selection)) { return Result.Failure<Unit, Error>(DomainErrors.Document.InvalidSelection); } See Also User Guide: Square Brackets Strategy Pattern Guide Developer Guide: Ribbon Item Rules

patterns_and_principles.html.title: Patterns and Principles | Developer Docs
patterns_and_principles.html.summary: Patterns and Principles Caution Adhere to Agreed Approaches Consistency is our primary driver for velocity. Do not change architectural approaches (e.g., swapping CQS for Services) without discussing it with the team first. Least Surprise > Clever Solutions. 1. Core Philosophy Least Surprise & Consistency We value code that looks boring. If you solve a problem in Module A, the solution in Module B should look identical. Tell-Don't-Ask Encapsulate logic within your Domain Models. Don't pull data out to process it; ask the object to do the work. ❌ Bad: Anemic Model var user = _repo.GetUser(id); if (user.IsActive && user.Expiry > DateTime.Now) { user.LastLogin = DateTime.Now; // Logic leaks into the service/handler _repo.Save(user); } ✅ Good: Rich Model var user = _repo.GetUser(id); var result = user.Login(); // Logic stays in the User entity if (result.IsSuccess) _repo.Save(user); 2. Architecture: Where Does This Code Belong? We follow a rigid dependency hierarchy. Use this decision tree: Is it a business rule or invariant? ├── Yes → Domain (Entity or Value Object) │ └── Does it need identity tracking? │ ├── Yes → Entity │ └── No → Value Object └── No → Continue... Is it orchestrating multiple domain objects? ├── Yes → Application (Command/Query Handler) └── No → Continue... Is it calling external systems (DB, API, files)? ├── Yes → Infrastructure └── No → Continue... Is it translating user input to commands? ├── Yes → Presentation (VSTO/Ribbon) └── No → Re-evaluate. Something's off. Layer Summary Layer Contains Dependencies Examples Domain Entities, Value Objects, Domain Events None EntitlementType, Template, Script Application Commands, Queries, Handlers, Interfaces Domain CreateDocument, GetScripts, IUserNotifier Infrastructure Implementations, DB, File System Application, Domain TemplateRepository, CommandDispatcher Presentation Ribbons, UI Application, Domain Thin dispatch only Quick Checks Domain has no dependencies → If you're adding a using for infrastructure, wrong layer Application orchestrates, doesn't decide → Business logic in handler = anemic domain smell Infrastructure implements interfaces → Defined in Application, implemented in Infrastructure Presentation is trivial → Just dispatch commands/queries, no logic Anti-patterns ❌ if (user.IsActive && user.HasPermission) in a handler → belongs in user.CanPerform() ❌ Repository interface in Infrastructure → belongs in Application ❌ COM objects in Application layer → stays in VSTO project 3. BDD: Before You Code Decision prompt: "Do I have a Gherkin scenario that describes what I'm building?" Answer Action No Write the scenario first. No exceptions. Yes, but vague Refine until Given/When/Then are concrete and testable Yes, specific Proceed to red → green → refactor Heuristics Scenario should be writable without knowing implementation If you can't write the scenario, you don't understand the requirement One scenario = one behavior (not one feature) Anti-patterns ❌ Writing tests after code "to get coverage" ❌ Scenarios that describe UI clicks, not behavior ❌ Multiple When clauses (that's multiple scenarios) 4. CQS: Command or Query? Decision prompt: "Does this operation change state?" Answer Type Returns Yes Command Result or Result<T> No Query Result<T> with data, no side effects Commands Can Read Internally From CreateDocument: [RequireEntitlement(EntitlementType.WordDocumentManagement)] [RequireEntitlement(EntitlementType.WordStandardTools)] public class Handler( ILogger<Handler> logger, ITemplateRepository templateRepository, // reads IDocumentManagementService documentManagementService, IDocumentRepository documentRepository, IUserNotifier userNotifier, // notifies IApplicationEventDispatcher applicationEventDispatcher) : ICommandHandler<Command> { public Result Handle(Command command, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) { var result = templateRepository.GetByTag(command.Value); // internal read if (!result.HasValue) { userNotifier.Alert(error, "Template Error"); // notify on failure return Result.Failure(error); } var doc = documentManagementService.CreateDocument(result.Value); // mutate applicationEventDispatcher.DispatchAsync( new DocumentCreatedFromTemplateEvent(doc.Value, template)); // event return doc; } } Queries Stay Pure From GetScripts: public class Handler(IScriptRepository repository) : IQueryHandler<Query, IEnumerable<Script>> { public Result<IEnumerable<Script>> Handle(Query query, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) { return Result.Success(repository.GetScripts()); // read only } } Rules Commands read what they need internally IUserNotifier in command handler keeps notification testable Queries have no side effects Use domain events for cross-aggregate coordination Anti-patterns ❌ Separate query dispatch just to read data for a command ❌ Query that writes audit logs → use decorator or event ❌ Notification logic in Ribbon → untestable 5. Design Principles (SOLID) Single Responsibility (SRP) Handlers should do one thing. ❌ Bad UpdateUserHandler: Validates input, checks DB, updates User, sends Email, logs to file. ✅ Good UpdateUserHandler: loads User, calls user.Update(), saves. Separately: UserUpdatedEvents trigger a SendEmailEventHandler. Open/Closed (OCP) Extend behavior without modifying existing code. ❌ Bad if (type == "Word") ExportWord(); else if (type == "PDF") ExportPdf(); // Modifying this method every time we add a format. ✅ Good // Inject IEnumerable<IExportStrategy> _strategies.First(s => s.Supports(type)).Export(); // Add new class PdfExportStrategy : IExportStrategy. No change to caller. Liskov Substitution (LSP) Subtypes must be substitutable for their base types. ❌ Bad public class ReadOnlyList : IList -> throws NotSupportedException on Add(). Why? code expecting an IList assumes it can Add. ✅ Good Use IEnumerable or IReadOnlyCollection if the list shouldn't be modified. 6. Coding Standards Naming Conventions Remove ambiguity. Commands: <Verb><Noun> → CreateDocument, RunScript, ActivateLicence Queries: Get<Noun> / Find<Noun> → GetScripts, GetLicence ❌ Bad DocumentManager (What does it do?) ProcessData (How?) ✅ Good ITemplateRepository SaveDocument Logging Log for the poor soul debugging this at 3 AM. ❌ Bad catch (Exception ex) { _logger.LogError("Error happened"); // What error? What context? } ✅ Good catch (Exception ex) { _logger.LogError(ex, "Failed to create document from template {TemplateName}", template.Name); } When to Log: Info: Significant business events ("Document created successfully"). Warning: Recoverable issues ("File locked, retrying"). Error: Exceptions/Failures that stop the flow. Commits Follow Conventional Commits. ❌ Bad "fixed bug" "updates" "wip" ✅ Good fix(templates): handle missing template gracefully feat(scripting): add custom property commands chore: update nuget packages 7. Specific Patterns Factories vs DI ❌ Bad Creating a factory just to call new. public class ServiceFactory { public Service Create() => new Service(); // Just register Service with DI! } ✅ Good Runtime decisions. public IExporter Create(string format) { return format switch { "PDF" => new PdfExporter(), "Word" => new WordExporter(), _ => throw new NotSupportedException() }; } Records vs Classes Type Use Case Rule Record DTOs, Messages, Config Use when data is the value. new Point(1,2) == new Point(1,2). Class Domain Entities Use when identity matters. Further Reading These sources informed our principles. Links may change; the principles above are authoritative for this project. Area Source BDD Gojko Adzic, Specification by Example (2011) Gherkin Cucumber Team, BDD with Cucumber - cucumber.io/docs/bdd DDD Vaughn Vernon, Domain-Driven Design Distilled (2016) DDD Tactical Eric Evans, Domain-Driven Design (2003) - "The Blue Book" CQS Martin Fowler, "Command Query Separation" - martinfowler.com/bliki Clean Architecture Robert C. Martin, Clean Architecture (2017)
